title: 10 possible side effects of Alpinia officinarum (Galangal) if used improperly
intro: Alpinia officinarum, also known as lesser galangal, is a plant commonly used in traditional medicine, but if used improperly, it can have several potential negative health effects. First, overconsumption may lead to digestive problems such as stomach discomfort, nausea, and diarrhea due to its stimulant effect on the digestive system. Second, it may cause allergic reactions, including skin rashes, hives, or difficulty breathing, especially in individuals sensitive to related plants like ginger or turmeric. Third, it might interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, and lead to increased risk of bleeding. Fourth, high doses could affect the nervous system, leading to restlessness, insomnia, or in severe cases, seizures. Lastly, long-term use or excessive intake might harm the liver, as some studies suggest that certain compounds in Alpinia officinarum might have hepatotoxic effects.
